MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTIAN GROH, MICHAEL BLUE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Defendants Michael Blue and Christian Groh's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied.
Movants' four arguments are unavailing.

First, movants' assertion that they cannot be sued as "Defendants" (Cmplt. 1:19-22) is supported by no authority. In any event, fraud by movants is also specified using their names. (Id. at 7:18-8:6, 9:15-10:2.)

Second, movants argue that their alleged fraudulent statements regarded future events. But the complaint alleges false statements by movants in the context of the sales transactions themselves. (Id. at 17:18-8:2.) Even if all the false statements were made earlier, the complaint pleads they were already "false when made" - i.e., movants knew defendants would not fully perform. (Id. at 9:19-20.)

Third, the complaint repeatedly alleges a pre-existing "plan" by movants and their confederates to acquire plaintiffs' businesses for less than full value. (Id. at 9:11-14, 9:27-10:2.) That plaintiffs were eventually paid a fraction of what they were owed does not negate this plan. (See Mot. 5:9-15.)

Fourth, declaratory relief is often pled in the alternative to other claims; whether or not it is duplicative is developed in the litigation.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
